IV. of the worship of God , the public form of liturgy , the sacrifice and sacrament of the lord supper . touch the worship of God , Sedulius deliver this general rule : that adorare alium praeter patrem and filium , and spiritum sanctum , impietatis crimen est . Sedul . in Rom. 1. to adore any other beside the father , and the son , and the holy ghost , be the crime of impiety ; and that totum quod debet deo anima , si alicui praeter deum reddiderit , moechatur . Id. in Rom. 2. all that the soul owe unto God , if it bestow it upon any beside God , it commit adultery . more particular , in the matter of image , recedentes à lumine veritatis sapiente ; quasi qui invenissent , quo modo invisibilis deus per simulacrum visibile coleretur . Id. in .
